data.name: Garlic Herb Chicken Thighs with Roasted Vegetables and Rice
data.instructions:
1. Rinse 2 cups of rice under cold water until the water runs mostly clear. Add the rice, 4 cups of water, and 1/2 tsp salt to a pot or rice cooker. Cook according to package instructions, then fluff and let cool slightly.
2. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Line two large baking trays with parchment paper if desired.
3. Cut 3 medium carrots into coins, 1 large red bell pepper into strips, 1 large zucchini into half-moons, and 1 red onion into wedges. Place all the vegetables on one of the trays. Drizzle with 2 tbsp olive oil, sprinkle with 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp black pepper, and 1 tsp dried Italian seasoning, and toss to coat. Spread into an even layer.
4. Pat 2 lb boneless skinless chicken thighs dry with paper towels. In a large bowl, mix 2 tbsp olive oil, 1.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp black pepper, 1.5 tsp garlic powder, 1 tsp onion powder, 1 tsp smoked paprika, 1 tsp dried oregano, and 1 tsp dried thyme. Add the chicken thighs and toss until evenly coated. Arrange the chicken on the second tray in a single layer.
5. Place both trays in the oven. Roast the vegetables for 20–25 minutes, stirring once halfway, until tender and lightly browned. Roast the chicken for 20–25 minutes, or until cooked through and the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
6. Optional: Squeeze the juice of 1/2 lemon over the cooked chicken thighs.
7. To assemble, divide the cooked rice among 6 meal-prep containers. Top each portion with roasted vegetables and 1–2 chicken thighs. Let everything cool to room temperature before sealing and refrigerating. Reheat in the microwave before serving.

data.name: Lemon Pepper Chicken with Broccoli and Potatoes
data.instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a large baking tray with parchment paper.
2. Cut 1.5 lb small potatoes into bite-size chunks. Cut 3 cups broccoli florets from a head of broccoli. Place potatoes and broccoli on the tray.
3. In a small bowl, mix 3 tbsp olive oil, 1.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp black pepper, 2 tsp lemon pepper seasoning, and 1 tsp garlic powder.
4. Add 2 lb boneless skinless chicken breasts to a large bowl and spoon over half of the oil mixture, tossing to coat. Arrange the chicken on the tray.
5. Toss the potatoes and broccoli in the remaining oil mixture and spread into an even layer around the chicken.
6. Bake for 25–30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the potatoes are tender, turning the vegetables once halfway.
7. Let cool slightly, then slice the chicken and divide everything into meal-prep containers.

data.name: Smoky Paprika Chicken and Sweet Potato Bake
data.instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Lightly oil a large baking dish.
2. Peel and cube 2 large sweet potatoes. Slice 1 red onion into wedges. Add both to the baking dish.
3. In a small bowl, whisk together 3 tbsp olive oil, 2 tsp smoked paprika, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1 tsp dried oregano, 1.5 tsp salt, and 1 tsp black pepper.
4. Add 2 lb boneless skinless chicken thighs to the dish and pour the oil mixture over the chicken and vegetables. Toss everything until coated and spread into an even layer.
5. Bake for 30–35 minutes, stirring the vegetables once halfway, until the chicken is cooked through and the sweet potatoes are tender.
6. Cool slightly, then portion into containers for easy reheating.

data.name: Italian Chicken and Green Bean Tray Bake
data.instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a large baking tray with parchment paper.
2. Trim 3 cups green beans and halve 1 pint cherry tomatoes. Slice 1 small red onion into wedges. Place vegetables on the tray.
3. In a bowl, combine 2 lb chicken drumsticks or thighs, 3 tbsp olive oil, 2 tsp Italian seasoning, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1.5 tsp salt, and 1 tsp black pepper. Toss to coat.
4. Arrange the chicken pieces on the tray among the vegetables.
5. Bake for 30–35 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender, stirring the vegetables once.
6. Cool slightly and portion into containers with some of each component.

data.name: Cumin Lime Chicken with Brown Rice
data.instructions:
1. Cook 2 cups brown rice according to package instructions with 4 cups water and 1/2 tsp salt. Fluff and set aside.
2. In a bowl, whisk together 3 tbsp olive oil, juice of 2 limes, 2 tsp ground cumin, 1.5 tsp chili powder, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1.5 tsp salt, and 1 tsp black pepper.
3. Add 2 lb boneless skinless chicken breasts cut into strips and toss to coat.
4. Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook the chicken in batches for 5–6 minutes, stirring occasionally, until cooked through and slightly browned.
5. Steam or quickly sauté 3 cups mixed bell pepper strips in a little olive oil and salt until crisp-tender.
6. Portion the brown rice, chicken, and peppers into containers and garnish with extra lime wedges if desired.

data.name: Honey Garlic Chicken and Carrots
data.instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Lightly oil a baking dish large enough to hold the chicken in a single layer.
2. Peel and slice 4 medium carrots into thick coins and spread them in the dish.
3. In a small bowl, whisk together 3 tbsp low-sodium soy sauce, 3 tbsp honey, 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp minced garlic, and 1/2 tsp black pepper.
4. Add 2 lb boneless skinless chicken thighs to the dish and pour the sauce over the chicken and carrots, turning to coat.
5. Bake for 30–35 minutes, basting once with the pan juices, until the chicken is cooked through and the carrots are tender.
6. Serve with cooked rice or quinoa and divide into containers.

data.name: Greek Chicken with Peppers and Onions
data.instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking tray with parchment.
2. Slice 2 bell peppers and 1 red onion into strips and place on the tray.
3. In a bowl, mix 3 tbsp olive oil, juice of 1 lemon, 2 tsp dried oregano, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1.5 tsp salt, and 1 tsp black pepper.
4. Add 2 lb chicken breasts or thighs and coat well. Arrange the chicken on the tray among the vegetables.
5. Bake for 25–30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and vegetables are tender.
6. Optional: Top with a sprinkle of crumbled feta after baking if dairy is acceptable.
7. Portion into containers with your choice of cooked rice or potatoes.

data.name: BBQ Baked Chicken with Corn and Green Beans
data.instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Lightly grease a large baking dish.
2. Arrange 2 lb bone-in chicken thighs or legs in the dish.
3. In a bowl, mix 1/2 cup BBQ sauce, 2 tbsp water, and 1 tbsp olive oil. Pour over the chicken and turn to coat.
4. Add 2 cups frozen corn and 2 cups green beans around the chicken. Season vegetables lightly with salt and pepper.
5. Bake for 35–40 minutes, basting the chicken once, until the chicken is cooked through and vegetables are tender.
6. Serve with cooked rice or potatoes and portion into containers.

data.name: Turmeric Chicken and Cauliflower Rice Bowls
data.instructions:
1. In a bowl, mix 2 tsp ground turmeric, 1 tsp ground cumin, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp black pepper, and 3 tbsp olive oil.
2. Add 2 lb chicken breast strips and toss to coat. Let marinate while you prepare the vegetables.
3. Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and cook the chicken for 6–8 minutes until cooked through.
4. In another skillet, sauté 4 cups riced cauliflower in 1 tbsp olive oil with a pinch of salt and pepper for 5–7 minutes until tender.
5. Slice 1 cucumber and 1 bell pepper for fresh crunch.
6. Assemble bowls or containers with cauliflower rice, chicken, and fresh vegetables.

data.name: Simple Roast Chicken Breasts with Mixed Vegetables
data.instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a large tray with parchment paper.
2. Place 2 lb chicken breasts on the tray. Surround with 2 cups baby carrots, 2 cups broccoli florets, and 2 cups halved Brussels sprouts.
3. In a bowl, whisk 3 tbsp olive oil, 1.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp black pepper, and 1 tsp garlic powder. Drizzle over the chicken and vegetables and toss gently.
4. Roast for 25–30 minutes, turning vegetables once, until the chicken is cooked through and vegetables are tender.
5. Slice chicken if desired and divide into meal-prep containers.

data.name: Herb Roasted Pork Loin with Carrots and Potatoes
data.instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Lightly oil a roasting pan.
2. Place a 2.5 lb pork loin in the center of the pan.
3. In a bowl, combine 3 tbsp olive oil, 2 tsp dried rosemary, 2 tsp dried thyme, 1.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp black pepper, and 2 cloves minced garlic. Rub the mixture all over the pork.
4. Cut 4 medium carrots and 4 medium potatoes into chunks and toss them with 1 tbsp olive oil, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1/2 tsp pepper. Arrange around the pork.
5. Roast for 45–55 minutes, or until the pork reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C) and the vegetables are tender, stirring vegetables once.
6. Let the pork rest 10 minutes before slicing. Portion slices with vegetables into containers.
